ConstructorArguments: Set the navgiation a dirty, so it will rebuild over the next few frames. If you need an immediate rebuild, call NavMesh.Generate(PhysicsWorld) instead.

Documentation.Summary:
Set the navgiation a dirty, so it will rebuild over the next few frames.
If you need an immediate rebuild, call <see cref="M:Sandbox.Navigation.NavMesh.Generate(Sandbox.PhysicsWorld)" /> instead.

ConstructorArguments: Generates or regenerates the navmesh tile at the given world position. This function is thread safe but can only be called from the main thread.

Documentation.Summary:
Generates or regenerates the navmesh tile at the given world position.
This function is thread safe but can only be called from the main thread.
Documentation.Remarks:
While most of the generation happens in parallel, this function also requires some time on the main thread.
If you need to update many tiles, consider spreading the updates accross multiple frames.

ConstructorArguments: Generates or regenerates the navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds. This function is thread safe but can only be called from the main thread.

Documentation.Summary:
Generates or regenerates the navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds.
This function is thread safe but can only be called from the main thread.
Documentation.Remarks:
While most of the generation happens in parallel, this function also requires some time on the main thread.
If you need to update many tiles, consider spreading the updates accross multiple frames.

ConstructorArguments: Removes the navmesh tile at the given world position.

Documentation.Summary: Removes the navmesh tile at the given world position.

ConstructorArguments: Removes all navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds.

Documentation.Summary: Removes all navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds.

ConstructorArguments: Queues the navmesh tile at the given world position for incremental generation over subsequent frames. Fire-and-forget alternative to NavMesh.GenerateTile(PhysicsWorld,Vector3).

Documentation.Summary:
Queues the navmesh tile at the given world position for incremental generation
over subsequent frames. Fire-and-forget alternative to <see cref="M:Sandbox.Navigation.NavMesh.GenerateTile(Sandbox.PhysicsWorld,Vector3)" />.

ConstructorArguments: Queues all navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds for incremental generation over subsequent frames. Fire-and-forget alternative to NavMesh.GenerateTiles(PhysicsWorld,BBox).

Documentation.Summary:
Queues all navmesh tiles overlapping with the given bounds for incremental generation
over subsequent frames. Fire-and-forget alternative to <see cref="M:Sandbox.Navigation.NavMesh.GenerateTiles(Sandbox.PhysicsWorld,BBox)" />.

ConstructorArguments: Computes a navigation path between the specified start and target positions on the navmesh. Uses the same pathfinding algorithm as NavMeshAgent, taking agent configuration into account if provided. The result is suitable for direct use with NavMeshAgent.SetPath(NavMeshPath). If a complete path cannot be found, the result may indicate an incomplete or failed path.

Documentation.Summary:
Computes a navigation path between the specified start and target positions on the navmesh.
Uses the same pathfinding algorithm as <see cref="T:Sandbox.NavMeshAgent" />, taking agent configuration into account if provided.
The result is suitable for direct use with <see cref="M:Sandbox.NavMeshAgent.SetPath(Sandbox.Navigation.NavMeshPath)" />.
If a complete path cannot be found, the result may indicate an incomplete or failed path.

ConstructorArguments: Get a random point on the navmesh, within the bounding box. This will return null if it can't find a point on the navmesh in a few tries. Returning false doesn't mean it's impossible, our algorithm here isn't the best.

Documentation.Summary:
Get a random point on the navmesh, within the bounding box. 
This will return null if it can't find a point on the navmesh in a few tries. Returning false doesn't mean it's impossible, our algorithm here isn't the best.

ConstructorArguments: Get a random point on the navmesh, within the sphere. This will return null if it can't find a point on the navmesh in a few tries. Returning false doesn't mean it's impossible, our algorithm here isn't the best.

Documentation.Summary:
Get a random point on the navmesh, within the sphere.
This will return null if it can't find a point on the navmesh in a few tries. Returning false doesn't mean it's impossible, our algorithm here isn't the best.
